title: Game Loop — Control Flow
description: Create the main game loop using if/elif/else statements. Write a process_command(command, player_state, rooms) function that takes a command string and returns a result string. Support these commands: 'look' (show current room description), 'go <room>' (move to a room), 'inventory' (list inventory items), 'status' (show name and health), and 'quit' (return 'QUIT'). Unknown commands return 'Unknown command'.
test_code:
assert callable(process_command), 'process_command should be a function'
test_state = {'name': 'Tester', 'health': 80, 'room': 'entrance', 'inventory': ['sword']}
test_rooms = {'entrance': 'The entrance.', 'hallway': 'A hallway.', 'vault': 'A vault.'}
look_result = process_command('look', test_state, test_rooms)
assert 'entrance' in look_result.lower() or 'The entrance' in look_result, f"'look' should describe current room, got: {look_result}"
go_result = process_command('go hallway', test_state, test_rooms)
assert test_state['room'] == 'hallway', f"room should be 'hallway' after 'go hallway', got {test_state['room']}"
bad_go = process_command('go dungeon', test_state, test_rooms)
assert test_state['room'] == 'hallway', 'Room should not change for invalid destination'
inv_result = process_command('inventory', test_state, test_rooms)
assert 'sword' in inv_result.lower(), f"inventory should mention 'sword', got: {inv_result}"
status_result = process_command('status', test_state, test_rooms)
assert 'Tester' in status_result or 'tester' in status_result.lower(), f"status should include player name, got: {status_result}"
assert '80' in status_result, f"status should include health, got: {status_result}"
quit_result = process_command('quit', test_state, test_rooms)
assert quit_result == 'QUIT', f"quit should return 'QUIT', got: {quit_result}"
unknown_result = process_command('dance', test_state, test_rooms)
assert unknown_result == 'Unknown command', f"unknown command should return 'Unknown command', got: {unknown_result}"
acceptance_criteria: 'go <room>' updates player_state['room'] if the room exists
'inventory' lists items or says inventory is empty
hints: Use command.startswith('go ') to detect the go command, then command.split(' ', 1)[1] to get the room name
Check if the destination room is in the rooms dict before moving
